title: pseudogynoxys cummingii
intro: Pseudogynoxys cummingii, also known as Cupido or 'Cupido Daisy', is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Andean region of South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudogynoxys cummingii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory infections, among other conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
